title: Ultrasound imaging
abstract: An ultrasound system includes a processor configured to cause the ultrasound system to: automatically detect (S325) when the ultrasound system starts a scan for an ultrasound examination; obtain (S380) a plurality of ultrasound images; track (S345) an amount of time the ultrasound system is scanning once the scan starts; generate (S370) a warning when the tracked amount of time exceeds a predetermined threshold; and display (S390) the tracked amount of time on a display overlaid on each of the plurality of ultrasound images.
claims: Claims Claim 1. An ultrasound system (100), comprising: a processor (152, 252) configured to: automatically detect (S325) when the ultrasound system starts a scan for an ultrasound examination; obtain (S380) a plurality of ultrasound images; track (S345) an amount of time the ultrasound system is scanning once the scan starts; issue (S370) a warning when the tracked amount of time exceeds a predetermined threshold; and display (S390), on a display, the plurality of ultrasound images with the tracked amount of time overlaid on each of the plurality of ultrasound images. Claim 2. The ultrasound system of claim 1, further comprising: an ultrasound cart (120) comprising the processor; and a display (180) configured to display the plurality of ultrasound images with the tracked amount of time overlaid on each of the plurality of ultrasound images. Claim 3. The ultrasound system of claim 1, further comprising: a portable transducer (213) comprising the processor, wherein the portable transducer is configured to link to an external device for the ultrasound examination via an application installed on the external device comprising the display. Claim 4. The ultrasound system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the processor is further configured to: identify (S330) a part of a body being scanned; and select the pre-determined threshold based on the identified part of the body being scanned. Claim 5. The ultrasound system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the automatic detection of when the ultrasound system starts a scan for an ultrasound examination is based on: automatically detecting (S320) when an ultrasound probe is placed on a body of a subject of the ultrasound examination. Claim 6. The ultrasound system of claim 5, wherein the processor is further configured to: apply a trained machine learning model to automatically detect when the ultrasound probe is placed on the body of the subject and to identify a part of the body being scanned; and reset the amount of time being tracked when the part of the body being scanned is changed. Claim 7. A computer-implemented method for ultrasound imaging, the method comprising: automatically detecting (S325) when an ultrasound system starts a scan for an ultrasound examination; receiving (S380) a plurality of ultrasound images; tracking (S345) an amount of time the ultrasound system is scanning once the scan starts; generating (S370) a warning when the tracked amount of time exceeds a predetermined threshold; and displaying (S390) on a display the plurality of ultrasound images with the tracked amount of time overlaid on each of the plurality of ultrasound images. Claim 8. The method of claim 7, further comprising: displaying the warning on a display. Claim 9. The method of claim 7 or 8, further comprising: receiving the predetermined threshold as a variable input set by a user. Claim 10. The method of any one of claims 7 to 9, further comprising: identifying (S330) a part of a body being scanned; and selecting the pre-determined threshold based on the identified part of the body being scanned. Claim 11. The method of any one of claims 7 to 10, wherein the step of automatically detecting when the ultrasound system starts a scan for an ultrasound examination is based on: automatically detecting (S320) when an ultrasound probe is placed on a body of a subject of the ultrasound examination. Claim 12. The method of any one of claims 7 to 11, further comprising: applying a trained machine learning model to automatically detect when the ultrasound probe is placed on the body of the subject and to identify a part of the body being scanned; and resetting the amount of time being tracked when the part of the body being scanned is changed. Claim 13. A computer program product that includes instructions, which when executed by a processor, cause the processor to carry out the method of any one of claims 7 to 12.
